Rhode Island Red.description: American breed known for reliable egg production and hardiness. Medium-sized birds with deep red plumage and red-brown eyes.
Rhode Island Red.cold_hardiness: Excellent — large comb can be susceptible to frostbite, but otherwise very hardy
Rhode Island Red.fun_fact: Rhode Island Reds were the state bird of Rhode Island (1954) and are one of the most productive brown-egg layers.

Speckled Sussex.description: British breed with distinctive mahogany and white speckled plumage. Curious, active foragers with excellent table manners (quiet compared to other breeds).
Speckled Sussex.temperament: Docile, curious, excellent foragers, friendly to humans
Speckled Sussex.cold_hardiness: Good — medium comb, adapts well to cold
Speckled Sussex.fun_fact: Speckled Sussex are known for being particularly friendly and following their owners around, almost dog-like in behavior.

Golden Laced Wyandotte.description: American breed with beautiful gold and black laced plumage. Compact, muscular birds with rose combs (frost-resistant) and yellow legs.
Golden Laced Wyandotte.temperament: Hardy, independent, can be broody, broody hens are excellent mothers
Golden Laced Wyandotte.cold_hardiness: Excellent — rose comb is frost-resistant, compact body, ideal for cold climates
Golden Laced Wyandotte.fun_fact: Wyandottes are excellent mothers and will often go broody, making them perfect for natural hatching.

Easter Egger.description: Not a standard breed but a hybrid type that lays colored eggs (blue, green, or pink). Variable plumage; typically have ear tufts or muffs and beards.
Easter Egger.fun_fact: Easter Eggers are prized by backyard farmers for their distinctive colored eggs. The 'Araucana' gene creates the blue egg color.

White Rock.description: American breed with white plumage and yellow legs. Large, friendly birds originally bred for meat production. Commonly crossed with other breeds.
White Rock.temperament: Friendly, calm, excellent mothers (when broody), good foragers
White Rock.cold_hardiness: Good — large comb can be susceptible to frostbite
White Rock.fun_fact: White Rocks were one of the first breeds to be widely used for commercial broiler production in the mid-20th century.

Black Australorp.description: Australian breed with glossy black plumage with a green sheen. Medium-sized, docile birds known for excellent egg production.
Black Australorp.temperament: Docile, friendly, good mothers (broody), excellent foragers
Black Australorp.cold_hardiness: Good — moderate comb, handles cold well
Black Australorp.fun_fact: Black Australorps hold the record for highest single-year egg production by a hen — 364 eggs in 365 days, set in the 1920s.

Easter Egger × Rhode Island Red Cross.description: Hybrid offspring combining Easter Egger's colored-egg genetics with RIR vigor. Inherits blue-egg production from EE parent and reliability from RIR.
Easter Egger × Rhode Island Red Cross.temperament: Friendly, curious, good foragers, benefits from hybrid vigor
Easter Egger × Rhode Island Red Cross.fun_fact: Homestead hybrids like this combine the best traits of both parents: reliable egg production with unique blue egg color.

White Broad-Breasted Turkey.description: The standard commercial turkey breed. Fast-growing, broad-chested birds raised primarily for meat. White plumage makes for clean processing.
White Broad-Breasted Turkey.temperament: Curious, social, can imprint on handlers when raised from poults
White Broad-Breasted Turkey.cold_hardiness: Moderate — need shelter from wind and wet, but tolerate cold if dry
White Broad-Breasted Turkey.fun_fact: Turkey poults will follow the first moving thing they see after hatching — making hand-raised poults remarkably tame.

Cream Legbar.description: British autosexing breed developed in the 1930s at Cambridge. Medium-sized birds with cream plumage and distinctive crests. Hens reliably lay blue eggs.
Cream Legbar.temperament: Active, alert, good foragers, can be flighty
Cream Legbar.cold_hardiness: Good — moderate comb, handles temperate climates well
Cream Legbar.fun_fact: Cream Legbars are one of the few breeds where you can tell males from females at hatch by their down color — an 'autosexing' trait.

flock_birds.name: Birdatha
flock_birds.age_note: One of the oldest birds on the farm
flock_birds.notes: Oldest hen on property, reliable producer. Lost in early April 2026.
flock_birds.cause_of_death: Predator losses — first week of April 2026

flock_birds.name: Birdgit
flock_birds.notes: Lost to hawk attack on 08-April-2026. Friendly and active, often seen exploring. Her namesake chick Birdadette carries on her legacy.

flock_birds.name: Henrietta
flock_birds.color_description: Gold and black laced plumage, purple leg band on left leg
flock_birds.notes: Identified by purple leg band; hatched with chick band

flock_birds.name: Little Big Red Junior
flock_birds.notes: Primary rooster; father of Birdadonna; visible in farm photos

flock_birds.name: Whitey Red Legs
flock_birds.breed: Easter Egger × Rhode Island Red cross (rooster)
flock_birds.color_description: White body with black-barred tail, yellow legs, likely male EE × RIR cross
flock_birds.notes: Secondary rooster; large single comb; mixed breed background

flock_birds.name: EE hen 1
flock_birds.color_description: Dark blue-black slate plumage, single comb
flock_birds.notes: Darker of the two Easter Egger hens; likely mother of Birdadonna

flock_birds.name: EE hen 2
flock_birds.color_description: Blue-gray plumage with yellow legs, single comb
flock_birds.notes: Lighter of the two Easter Egger hens; good forager

flock_birds.name: Birdadonna
flock_birds.age_note: Hatched April 2, 2025 — almost exactly 1 year old
flock_birds.color_description: Blue-gray body with golden auburn hackle
flock_birds.notes: Hatched in incubator on boss's desk; dam: EE hen 1, sire: Little Big Red Junior. Lost in early April 2026.
flock_birds.cause_of_death: Predator losses — first week of April 2026

flock_birds.name: Black Australorp hen
flock_birds.color_description: Black plumage with iridescent green sheen
flock_birds.notes: Excellent egg producer. Lost in early April 2026.
flock_birds.cause_of_death: Predator losses — first week of April 2026

flock_birds.name: Birdadette
flock_birds.age_note: Hatched April 6, 2026 in incubator on the boss's desk
flock_birds.temperament: Alert, curious — started learning computer skills on day two
flock_birds.notes: First successful incubator hatch of 2026. Hatched from a blue egg (set 16-March, ~21 day incubation). Named after Birdgit who was lost to a hawk two days later. Posed on the keyboard next to her cracked shell moments after hatching.

flock_birds.name: Turkey poults (3)
flock_birds.notes: 3 White Broad-Breasted turkey poults, about 1 week old when purchased. First turkeys on the farm.

flock_birds.name: Cream Legbar chicks (4)
flock_birds.color_description: Varied chick down (autosexing breed — males and females have different coloring)
flock_birds.notes: 4 Cream Legbar chicks, about 1 week old when purchased. Will lay blue eggs when mature. Autosexing breed — can tell gender from hatch.

flock_birds.name: Cackle Hatchery chicks (15)
flock_birds.breed: Mixed — Exotic Island Fowl Special (5) + Rare Chick Special (10)
flock_birds.age_note: Shipped 08-April-2026 from Cackle Hatchery, Lebanon MO (Order #519118)
flock_birds.color_description: Varied — assortment of rare and exotic breeds
flock_birds.notes: 15 chicks from Cackle Hatchery: 5 Exotic Island Fowl Special + 10 Rare Chick Special. Reinforcements after losing Birdgit. Exact breeds TBD as they feather out. Total order: $122.80 shipped USPS Priority.
